commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 19. He saith then, "But I as a deaf man heard not" (ver. 13). He who replied not to what He heard, did, as it were, not hear them. "But I as a deaf man heard not. And I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth." And he repeats the same things again.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that opens not his mouth. I utterly renounce all sin. (Worthington) David would make no reply to Semei, (Theodoret) nor our Saviour to Pilate. (Calmet) Silence is often the best defence. (Haydock) Eagerness to justify one's self, causes trouble and disedification. (Berthier) The prophet joins the deaf and dumb; as those who have naturally the former defect, are also affected with the latter. ) (Haydock).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
